segments.text: After these events, the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision:

Do not be afraid, Abram. I am your shield,
your very great reward.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: But Abram replied,
O Lord GOD, what can You give me, since I remain childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: Abram continued,
Behold, You have given me no offspring, so a servant in my household will be my heir.
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: Then the word of the LORD came to Abram, saying,
This one will not be your heir, but one who comes from your own body will be your heir.
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: And the LORD took him outside and said,
Now look to the heavens and count the stars, if you are able.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then He told him,
So shall your offspring be.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Abram believed the LORD, and it was credited to him as righteousness.[^72]
The LORD also told him,
I am the LORD, who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land to possess.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But Abram replied,
Lord GOD, how can I know that I will possess it?
segments.speaker: Abram

segments.text: And the LORD said to him,
Bring Me a heifer, a goat, and a ram, each three years old, along with a turtledove and a young pigeon.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: So Abram brought all these to Him, split each of them down the middle, and laid the halves opposite each other. The birds, however, he did not cut in half.
And the birds of prey descended on the carcasses, but Abram drove them away.
As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and suddenly great terror and darkness overwhelmed him.
Then the LORD said to Abram,
Know for certain that your descendants will be strangers in a land that is not their own, and they will be enslaved and mistreated four hundred years.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But I will judge the nation they serve as slaves, and afterward they will depart with many possessions.[^73]
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: You, however, will go to your fathers in peace and be buried at a ripe old age.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: In the fourth generation your descendants will return here, for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: When the sun had set and darkness had fallen, behold, a smoking firepot and a flaming torch appeared and passed between the halves of the carcasses.
On that day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying,
To your descendants I have given this land—from the river of Egypt to the great River Euphrates—
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: the land of the Kenites, Kenizzites, Kadmonites,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Hittites, Perizzites, Rephaites,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Amorites, Canaanites, Girgashites, and Jebusites.
segments.speaker: the Lord
